notes: Codex not applicable to shampoo; included for completeness — no action required

UNECE WP.29 covers motor vehicle safety regulations; not applicable to shampoo/cosmetics
notes: Not applicable; included for completeness

regulation_name: WADA Prohibited List (not applicable — cosmetic use only)
key_requirements: WADA Prohibited List covers sports doping; shampoo as cosmetic rinse-off not in scope unless specific prohibited substance claim
notes: Not directly applicable; monitor if cosmetic contains any listed substance

regulation_name: OECD Test Guidelines for Cosmetics Safety Assessment
key_requirements: OECD validated test methods for skin sensitisation, dermal absorption, eye irritation; referenced in EU, US, KR, JP safety assessments
notes: OECD TGs used for ingredient safety data; not a market access regulation but required for PIF/safety dossier

regulation_name: EU Cosmetics Regulation — Annex VI UV Filters
key_requirements: Positive list of 27 authorised UV filters; max concentrations; restriction conditions per filter entry; combination rules; CPNP notification required
notes: Annex VI is exhaustive: only listed UV filters permitted. Last substantive update 2022. SCCS opinions trigger Annex amendments.

regulation_name: EU Cosmetics Regulation — Nano UV filters (TiO2, ZnO)
key_requirements: TiO2 nano allowed ≤25% (face/body, not spray) with specific nano notification in CPNP; ZnO nano allowed ≤25%; nano labeling [nano] after INCI name required
notes: Spray forms with TiO2 nano prohibited due to inhalation risk (Commission Regulation EU 2016/1143). ZnO nano SCCS 2021 opinion confirmed safe ≤25%.

enforcement_body: EC Scientific Committee on Consumer Safety
key_requirements: Safety opinions on individual UV filters; may trigger Annex VI amendments; oxybenzone (benzophenone-3) max concentration reviewed; homosalate reduced to 7.34%
notes: Homosalate Regulation (EU) 2022/1531 reduced max from 10% to 7.34%. Oxybenzone SCCS 2021: safe ≤2.2% — Annex VI amendment pending. Monitor SCCS work programme.

regulation_name: EU Regulation on REACH — UV filter ingredients
key_requirements: Chemical safety assessment for UV filter substances used above 1 tonne/year; SVHCs impacting formulation; restriction proposals under Annex XVII
notes: REACH restrictions on benzophenone-3 and ethylhexyl methoxycinnamate under review for endocrine disruption. Monitor ECHA ED screening.

regulation_name: EU Marine Environment — Draft restriction proposal on chemical UV filters
key_requirements: Potential restriction on oxybenzone and octinoxate in sunscreens due to aquatic toxicity; linked to marine protected area impacts
notes: No adopted EU-wide ban yet (unlike Hawaii/US-HI). ECHA evaluation ongoing. Reef-safe claims not yet regulated in EU but consumer pressure growing.

regulation_name: EU Cosmetics Regulation — Annex VI (applicable en FR)
key_requirements: Filtres UV: liste positive Annexe VI; notification CPNP; PIF en français si requis; étiquetage en français obligatoire
notes: Pas de liste nationale de filtres UV — Annexe VI EU s'applique directement. ANSM cosmétovigilance pour EIG.

regulation_ref: Code de la Santé Publique L5131-6 à L5131-10
key_requirements: Déclaration obligatoire d'effets indésirables graves (EIG) à l'ANSM; délai immédiat dès connaissance; suivi des signaux liés aux filtres UV (photoallergies)
notes: Les filtres UV (benzophenone-3, BMDBM) sont des sources fréquentes de photoallergie — suivi ANSM renforcé.

regulation_name: EU Cosmetics Regulation — Annex VI (applicable in DE)
key_requirements: UV filters positive list; German-language labeling; nano notification; SPF claim substantiation per Colipa/ISO method

regulation_name: BfR Recommendations on UV Filters — national guidance
key_requirements: BfR technical opinions on specific UV filters and nano particles; market surveillance on SPF label accuracy; endocrine disruption flags
notes: BfR has issued opinions on 4-MBC and OMC regarding endocrine activity; no national ban but industry vigilance required.

regulation_name: EU Cosmetics Regulation — Annex VI (applicable in IT)
key_requirements: UV filters positive list; Italian labeling; CPNP notification; nano labeling [nano]; SPF testing per ISO 24444
SPF claim guidance; labeling of SPF category (low/medium/high/very high); UVA claim requirements; caution against very high SPF marketing only
notes: Italy follows EU/Colipa SPF labeling recommendations. Circular reinforces mandatory UVA logo if UVA claim made.

regulation_name: EU Cosmetics Regulation — Annex VI (applicable in ES)
key_requirements: UV filters positive list; Spanish-language labeling; CPNP; PIF; nano declaration; SPF substantiation

regulation_name: AEMPS Guidance on Sunscreen SPF Claims
key_requirements: SPF minimum testing requirements; maximum SPF claim on label vs tested SPF ratio; UVA protection indication; reef-safe claims monitoring
notes: Spain particularly sensitive to reef-safe marketing claims in Balearic Islands and Canary Islands (marine protected areas).

regulation_name: EU Cosmetics Regulation — Annex VI (applicable in NL)
enforcement_body: NVWA (Netherlands Food and Consumer Product Safety Authority)
key_requirements: UV filters positive list; Dutch-language labeling; CPNP; nano labeling; SPF testing per ISO 24444; UVA per ISO 24442

regulation_name: UK Cosmetics Regulation — Annex VI UV Filters (post-Brexit)
enforcement_body: OPSS (Office for Product Safety and Standards) / MHRA
key_requirements: UK-retained Annex VI positive list (mirrors EU list but may diverge post-Brexit); UKCPNP notification; UK Responsible Person; English-language labeling; nano labeling [nano]
notes: Post-Brexit GB regulation mirrors EU 1223/2009 Annex VI as of exit day. OPSS managing divergence — monitor for UK-specific UV filter amendments. Northern Ireland follows EU rules under Windsor Framework.

regulation_name: UK REACH — UV filter substance restrictions
key_requirements: UK-retained REACH restrictions; UK HSE managing substance evaluation independently post-Brexit; potential divergence on ED criteria for UV filters
notes: UK REACH diverging from EU REACH on endocrine disruptor criteria. UV filters like 4-MBC and BP-3 under scrutiny.

regulation_name: Swiss Cosmetics Regulation (LPTh / OPCos) — UV Filters
regulation_ref: Ordonnance sur les produits cosmétiques (OPCos) SR 817.023.31
key_requirements: Positive list of UV filters aligned with EU Annex VI but updated independently; Swiss notification obligation; Responsible Person in CH or EU; nano labeling required
notes: CH typically adopts EU Annex VI changes with 6-18 month delay. Check Swissmedic database for current Swiss Annex VI. TiO2 nano spray ban applies.

key_requirements: Chemical restrictions mirroring EU REACH; endocrine disruptors under evaluation; benzophenone-3 monitoring

regulation_ref: Forskrift om kosmetikk og kroppspleieprodukter (EEA Joint Committee Decision 2013/60)
key_requirements: Norway implements EU 1223/2009 including Annex VI via EEA Agreement; UV filter positive list; CPNP notification in Norwegian if required; nano labeling
notes: EEA: Norway adopts EU cosmetics regulations with minor delay. Norwegian notification system aligned with CPNP.

regulation_name: EAEU Technical Regulation on Cosmetics (TR CU 009/2011) — UV filters
key_requirements: EAEU positive list of UV filters (List 6 of TR CU 009); concentration limits; SPF testing per GOST methods or EAEU-accepted ISO; Cyrillic labeling required; registration in EAEU database
notes: EAEU list of UV filters differs from EU Annex VI — some filters allowed in EAEU not allowed in EU and vice versa. Sunscreens registered as cosmetics in EAEU. Western sanctions may complicate registration logistics.

enforcement_body: TITCK (Turkish Medicines and Medical Devices Agency)
key_requirements: Turkish positive list of UV filters aligned with EU Annex VI; notification in e-TUBIM system; Turkish-language labeling; Responsible Person in Turkey or EU; nano requirements
notes: Turkey implements EU cosmetics framework with some delay. Sunscreens regulated as cosmetics. TITCK notification mandatory before market entry.

regulation_name: Ukrainian Cosmetics Technical Regulation — UV Filters
regulation_ref: Technical Regulation on Cosmetics (CMU Decree No. 65, 2013 + updates)
key_requirements: Positive list of UV filters aligned with EU; state registration for sunscreens; Ukrainian-language labeling; safety assessment
notes: Ukraine aligning with EU acquis under EU Association Agreement. War context: enforcement capacity limited but regulatory framework maintained. Sunscreens may require state registration (not just notification).

regulation_name: EAEU TR CU 009/2011 — Cosmetics Technical Regulation (UV filters)
key_requirements: Positive list of UV filters in Annex 6; max concentrations; single EAEU registration valid in RU, BY, KZ, AM, KG; Cyrillic or national language labeling; SPF claim per GOST R 52343 or ISO 24444
notes: EAEU UV filter list includes some filters not in EU Annex VI (e.g. Mexoryl SX has different status). Registration in FSIS (Federal Service information system). Members: Russia, Belarus, Kazakhstan, Armenia, Kyrgyzstan.

regulation_name: FDA Sunscreen OTC Drug Monograph — 21 CFR Part 352 / Proposed Order
regulation_ref: 21 CFR Part 352 (historical) + FDA Proposed Order (NPRM 2019 + 2021 update)
enforcement_body: FDA (Center for Drug Evaluation and Research — CDER)
key_requirements: Sunscreens classified as OTC drugs (not cosmetics) in US; only 2 UV filters GRASE (zinc oxide, titanium dioxide); 12 filters need more safety data; avobenzone conditionally GRASE; active ingredient limits; SPF testing per FDA-specified methods; labeling per 21 CFR 201 and drug facts panel required; SPF 2-50+ range
notes: Critical divergence from EU: US treats sunscreens as OTC drugs. GRASE status: ZnO and TiO2 = GRASE Category I. PABA and trolamine salicylate = NOT GRASE. 12 filters (including oxybenzone, avobenzone pending) need additional data per Sunscreen Innovation Act. Hawaii state ban on oxybenzone/octinoxate (Act 104, 2018, effective Jan 2021) — note in US row.

regulation_name: FDA Sunscreen Innovation Act (SIA 2014) — New filter applications
key_requirements: Created GRASE determination process for new UV filter applications (TEA applications); 8 pending TEA applications for EU-allowed filters (Tinosorb M/S, Mexoryl SX/XL, etc.); FDA has not approved any new filters since 1999
notes: Major US regulatory gap: many EU-approved UV filters (Tinosorb, Mexoryl, Bemotrizinol etc.) not available for US sunscreens. Formulation must be adapted for US market. SIA aimed to accelerate approvals but no new filters approved as of 2025.

enforcement_body: Hawaii Dept of Land and Natural Resources + DHHL
key_requirements: Ban on sale of sunscreens containing oxybenzone (benzophenone-3) and octinoxate (ethylhexyl methoxycinnamate) without prescription; civil penalties up to $1000 per violation
notes: State-specific (Hawaii). First US state sunscreen ingredient ban. US Virgin Islands similar ban. Coral reef protection. Affects formulation strategy for US market if selling in Hawaii.

regulation_name: FDA Drug Labeling — Drug Facts Panel (sunscreen OTC)
regulation_ref: 21 CFR 201.66 (Drug Facts labeling for OTC drugs)
key_requirements: Drug Facts panel mandatory on all OTC sunscreen labels: active ingredients with % concentration; purpose (sunscreen); uses; warnings (keep out of eyes, stop use if rash); directions (apply 15 min before sun exposure; reapply after 80 min swimming/sweating); inactive ingredients
notes: Sunscreen label in US fundamentally different from cosmetics (no INCI list in same format — drug facts panel instead). Critical compliance difference vs EU/AU/etc.

regulation_name: FDA Water Resistance and Broad Spectrum Claims — OTC Sunscreen
key_requirements: Water resistance claim requires 40-min or 80-min test per ISO 16474; broad spectrum claim requires Critical Wavelength ≥370nm (SPF:UVA ratio test); no more sweatproof/waterproof/sunblock claims
notes: US broad spectrum testing method differs from EU UVA test (ISO 24442). Companies must run US-specific tests for FDA claims.

regulation_ref: California Health & Safety Code §25249.5 et seq. (Prop 65)
key_requirements: Warning required if product exposes consumers to listed carcinogens/reproductive toxicants above safe harbor levels; oxybenzone (benzophenone-3) and certain sunscreen chemicals under review for ED listing
notes: Titanium dioxide (in spray form) listed as Prop 65 carcinogen — spray sunscreens with TiO2 nano may require Prop 65 warning. Oxybenzone under OEHHA review for reproductive toxicity listing.

regulation_name: California Cleaning Product Right to Know Act (sunscreen adjacent)
key_requirements: Online ingredient disclosure for personal care products sold in California; some sunscreen active ingredients subject to disclosure
notes: Primarily cosmetics/personal care — applies to sunscreen products sold as cosmetics. Sunscreen OTC drugs may have different scope. Monitor DTSC guidance.

regulation_name: New York Safer Beauty Bill Package (S4573/A7648 — draft)
regulation_ref: NY S4573 / A7648 (proposed; various session years)
key_requirements: Proposed restrictions on certain chemicals in personal care products including sunscreens; would require disclosure of fragrance allergens and restrict some UV-adjacent substances; not yet enacted as of 2025
notes: NY does not have enacted sunscreen-specific OTC state law. Federal FDA OTC monograph preempts most state sunscreen drug regulation. Monitor NY legislative session.

regulation_ref: Sunscreen Monograph (Natural Health Products Directorate + Drug Branch) — updated 2023
enforcement_body: Health Canada (NNHPD + Health Products and Food Branch)
key_requirements: Sunscreens in Canada regulated as either drugs (DIN required) or NHPs (NPN required) depending on concentration; Drug Identification Number mandatory for SPF claims; permitted UV filters list different from EU and US; SPF testing per Health Canada methods; Bilingual (EN/FR) labeling required
notes: Canada permits several UV filters not allowed in US (e.g. avobenzone, Tinosorb S/M) but not all EU-approved filters. Formulation must be verified against Health Canada permitted list. Sunscreen with SPF claim = drug = DIN required.

regulation_name: Cosmetic Regulations (Canada) — if SPF claim absent
regulation_ref: Cosmetic Regulations (SOR/2006-18) under Food and Drugs Act
enforcement_body: Health Canada — Consumer Product Safety Directorate
key_requirements: Without SPF drug claim: cosmetic notification required; ingredient listing per INCI; prohibited/restricted substances list; sunscreen ingredient still subject to safety assessment
notes: If marketed purely as moisturizer with UV protection (no SPF claim), may be regulated as cosmetic. Usually SPF claim triggers drug classification.

enforcement_body: COFEPRIS (Comisión Federal para la Protección contra Riesgos Sanitarios)
key_requirements: Labeling requirements for cosmetics including sunscreens; Spanish-language labels; SPF indication; ingredient list per INCI; manufacturer info; registration number

regulation_ref: Reglamento de Control Sanitario de Productos y Servicios (RCSP) + NOM-189-SSA1/SCFI-2002
key_requirements: Sunscreens require sanitary registration (registro sanitario) with COFEPRIS; UV filter list aligned broadly with international standards; SPF testing documentation required; 5-year registration renewal
notes: Mexico treats sunscreens as cosmetics regulated by COFEPRIS, not as drugs. Registro sanitario process differs significantly from US OTC Drug approval.

regulation_ref: Resolução da Diretoria Colegiada RDC No. 30/2012 (ANVISA)
key_requirements: Sunscreens classified as category 2 cosmetics (notificação); positive list of UV filters; max concentrations; SPF mandatory claim; UVA/UVB protection; SPF testing per COLIPA/ISO methods; labeling in Portuguese; manufacturing GMP required
notes: Brazil regulates sunscreens as cosmetics (not drugs). ANVISA UV filter list is broader than US FDA but differs from EU. Annual notification fee. Nano ingredients require nano declaration.

key_requirements: Updated cosmetics ingredient list replacing previous resolution; prohibited and restricted substances; impacts on UV filter combinations and preservatives in sunscreen formulations
notes: RDC 752/2022 replaces RDC 29/2012 for ingredients. UV filter limits maintained; some new restrictions on combination formulations.

regulation_name: ANMAT Disposición 155/1998 + MERCOSUR Reglamento Técnico — Sunscreens
enforcement_body: ANMAT (Administración Nacional de Medicamentos, Alimentos y Tecnología Médica)
key_requirements: Sunscreens regulated as cosmetics; Mercosur-harmonized UV filter list; SPF claim mandatory; labeling in Spanish; sanitary registration with ANMAT; GMP required
notes: Argentina follows Mercosur Technical Regulation on sunscreens. UV filter list harmonized across Mercosur members. Registration (habilitación) required before marketing.

regulation_ref: Reglamento de Cosméticos (Decreto Supremo No. 239, MINSAL 2002 + updates)
key_requirements: Sanitary registration required; Mercosur-aligned UV filter list; SPF and UVA claims; Spanish labeling; GMP compliance; sunscreen categorized as cosmetic
notes: Chile follows Mercosur sunscreen framework. ISP registration process similar to Argentina/Brazil. UV filter list based on Mercosur GMC/RES 15/97 and updates.

regulation_name: INVIMA — Andean Community Cosmetics Decision 516 + Local NTC
enforcement_body: INVIMA (Instituto Nacional de Vigilancia de Medicamentos y Alimentos)
key_requirements: Andean Community harmonized cosmetics regulation; sunscreen notification or registration depending on risk level; UV filter list per CAN Decision 516 + INVIMA resolution; Spanish labeling; SPF testing
notes: Colombia applies Andean Community Decision 516 (not Mercosur). UV filter list may differ from Mercosur. INVIMA sanitary registration or notification required.

regulation_ref: GMC/RES Nº 15/97 + GMC/RES Nº 11/01 (Mercosur Grupo Mercado Común)
key_requirements: Harmonized positive list of UV filters for Mercosur member states (AR, BR, PY, UY); SPF testing methodology; labeling requirements; classification as cosmetics not drugs
notes: Mercosur UV filter list differs from EU Annex VI and US FDA. Some EU-approved filters not on Mercosur list. Paraguay and Uruguay apply same framework. Implemented individually by each country's regulatory authority.

regulation_name: CSAR — Cosmetics Supervision and Administration Regulation (Sunscreen)
regulation_ref: Regulations on Cosmetics Supervision and Administration (CSAR, 国务院令第727号, 2021)
key_requirements: Sunscreens classified as special cosmetics requiring registration (not notification); NMPA registration with full dossier including safety assessment, efficacy testing; UV filter list per NMPA Annex (HG/T standards); SPF testing per GB/T methods; Chinese-language labeling mandatory; 4-year registration term
notes: Critical: sunscreens = special cosmetics in China requiring registration (not simple notification). Registration takes 6-12 months. NMPA permits list for UV filters differs from EU — check updated NMPA Technical Guideline 2022.

regulation_name: NMPA Technical Guideline on UV Filters (2022)
regulation_ref: NMPA Guiding Principles for Safety and Efficacy Testing of Sunscreen Cosmetics (2022)
key_requirements: Updated UV filter permitted list; SPF testing per ISO 24444 (adopted in China as GB/T 35916); UVA testing per ISO 24442; in-vivo human testing often required; nano ingredients require additional NMPA notification
notes: NMPA updated UV filter list in 2022 Technical Guideline. Some EU-approved nano UV filters require separate nano safety assessment. China requires safety evaluation by China-registered assessor.

regulation_ref: NMPA Guiding Principles for Safety Assessment of Nano-materials in Cosmetics (2021)
key_requirements: Nano TiO2 and ZnO used as UV filters subject to nano safety assessment; particle size characterization required; specific physicochemical data; toxicology data for nano form
notes: China has specific nano cosmetics guidelines. TiO2 nano and ZnO nano in sunscreens require nano dossier component within CSAR registration.

regulation_name: Hong Kong Cosmetics Regulation — Sunscreen (unregistered cosmetic regime)
regulation_ref: Pharmacy and Poisons Ordinance (Cap 138) + Consumer Goods Safety Ordinance (Cap 456)
enforcement_body: Dept of Health + CITA (Customs and Excise Dept)
key_requirements: HK does not require pre-market registration for sunscreens as cosmetics; safety responsibility on importer/manufacturer; prohibited substances list aligned broadly with EU; sunscreens with pharmaceutical claims may trigger drug classification
notes: Hong Kong has no mandatory sunscreen registration system. However, products must not contain prohibited ingredients. Strong SPF/drug efficacy claims could trigger drug classification under Pharmacy & Poisons Ordinance.

regulation_name: Taiwan Cosmetics Hygiene and Safety Act — Sunscreen
regulation_ref: Cosmetics Hygiene and Safety Act (化粧品衛生安全管理法, 2019)
enforcement_body: Taiwan FDA (TFDA) under Ministry of Health and Welfare
key_requirements: Sunscreens with SPF claim classified as special-purpose cosmetics requiring registration (許可證); UV filter positive list per TFDA Annex; SPF testing per ISO 24444 or equivalent; Traditional Chinese labeling; TFDA registration before market entry
notes: Taiwan treats SPF-claiming sunscreens as special-purpose cosmetics (similar to CN). TFDA registration required. UV filter list differs slightly from EU — check current TFDA permitted list.

regulation_name: Japan Quasi-Drug (Iyakubugaiyahin) Regime — Sunscreen under PMD Act
regulation_ref: Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices Act (PMD Act, 薬機法) — Quasi-drugs
enforcement_body: PMDA + Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare (MHLW)
key_requirements: Sunscreens with SPF/UV protection claims regulated as quasi-drugs (医薬部外品); requires MHLW approval/notification; approved UV filter list (UV absorbers list in quasi-drug standard); SPF testing per Japanese Cosmetic Industry Association methods; Japanese labeling required; manufacturing license required
notes: Japan quasi-drug regime for sunscreens is distinct from EU cosmetics. UV filter list (JCIA approved list) has some differences from EU Annex VI. Products with only physical claims may be cosmetics. SPF claim = quasi-drug trigger.

regulation_name: Japan Quasi-Drug UV Absorber Permitted List
regulation_ref: MHLW Quasi-Drug Ingredient Standards (医薬部外品原料規格) + MHLW Ministerial Notification
key_requirements: Specific approved UV absorber list for quasi-drugs; chemical-specific standards; purity requirements; max use concentrations; some EU-approved UV filters not on Japanese quasi-drug list
notes: Key divergence: Japan quasi-drug UV absorber list (circa 28 substances) differs from EU Annex VI. Tinosorb S not approved in Japan as of 2024. Formulation must be adapted.

regulation_ref: MHLW Guidance on Safety Assessment of Nano-ingredients (2006 + updates)
key_requirements: Nano TiO2 and ZnO in sunscreens: safety data required; particle size distribution; dermal absorption data; inclusion in safety assessment dossier

regulation_ref: Cosmetics Act (화장품법) + Functional Cosmetics Review Standards
enforcement_body: Ministry of Food and Drug Safety (MFDS / 식품의약품안전처)
key_requirements: Sunscreens with UV protection claims classified as functional cosmetics requiring MFDS review/registration; UV filter positive list (별표 4); SPF testing per KS M 2503 or ISO 24444; UVA testing; Korean labeling mandatory; functional cosmetics registration certificate required
notes: Korea treats sunscreens as functional cosmetics requiring pre-market review (신고 or 심사 depending on whether using already-approved formula). UV filter list (별표 4) broadly aligned with COLIPA/ISO but has Korea-specific max concentrations.

regulation_name: Korea Functional Cosmetics UV Filter Permitted List (별표 4)
key_requirements: Specific permitted UV filter list with max concentrations for Korean market; includes organic and inorganic UV filters; nano TiO2 and ZnO with specific concentration limits; SPF range and claim requirements
notes: Korea별표 4 UV filter list: generally aligned with EU but some differences in max concentrations. Check MFDS database for current list. Nano ingredients require nano safety data.

regulation_name: Singapore Health Sciences Authority (HSA) — Cosmetics Notification
regulation_ref: Health Products Act + Cosmetic Products (Notification) Regulations
key_requirements: Sunscreens with SPF claim: product notification with HSA required; prohibited ingredient list aligned with EU/ASEAN; SPF claim must be substantiated; labeling in English; Singapore free trade zone facilitates regional distribution
notes: Singapore HSA follows ASEAN Cosmetics Directive framework. SPF sunscreens notified (not registered) unless drug claims made. Fast notification process — typically online self-declaration.

key_requirements: ASEAN harmonized cosmetics framework; positive list for sunscreens aligned with EU Annex VI; single notification for ASEAN mutual recognition in theory
notes: ASEAN MRA for cosmetics means notification in one ASEAN country should be recognized in others — but implementation varies. UV filter list in ASEAN ACD Annex VI broadly mirrors EU.

regulation_name: Malaysia NPRA — Control of Drugs and Cosmetics Regulations (Sunscreen)
regulation_ref: Control of Drugs and Cosmetics Regulations 1984 (CDCR 1984) + ASEAN ACD
enforcement_body: NPRA (National Pharmaceutical Regulatory Agency) under MOH
key_requirements: Sunscreens require product registration with NPRA (not just notification); UV filter list per ASEAN ACD Annex VI; SPF claim documentation; Bahasa Malaysia or English labeling; product registration certificate (Notifikasi Kosmetik) required
notes: Malaysia transitioned to ASEAN-aligned cosmetics notification system. Sunscreens with SPF claims require NPRA notification. UV filter positive list = ASEAN ACD Annex VI.

regulation_name: Thailand FDA — Cosmetics Act B.E. 2558 (2015) — Sunscreen
regulation_ref: Cosmetics Act B.E. 2558 (2015) + Thai FDA notifications
enforcement_body: Thai FDA (Food and Drug Administration, Ministry of Public Health)
key_requirements: Sunscreens as cosmetics require product notification with Thai FDA; UV filter positive list per Thai FDA Notification aligned with ASEAN ACD; SPF claim substantiation; Thai or English labeling; safety assessment required
notes: Thailand 2015 Cosmetics Act modernized the framework. UV filter list follows ASEAN ACD Annex VI. Sunscreens with SPF claim require prior notification. Nano ingredients must be declared.

regulation_ref: Circular 06/2011/TT-BYT (Ministry of Health) + ASEAN ACD implementation
enforcement_body: Vietnam Drug Administration (DAV) under Ministry of Health
key_requirements: Sunscreens as cosmetics: product declaration (công bố sản phẩm) with DAV; UV filter list per ASEAN ACD/Vietnam permitted list; SPF labeling; Vietnamese labeling required; GMP recommended; certificate of origin may be required for import
notes: Vietnam implements ASEAN ACD. Product declaration required before marketing. Local Responsible Person or authorized representative required. UV filter positive list aligned with ASEAN ACD Annex VI.

regulation_ref: Peraturan BPOM No. 12 Tahun 2019 (Cosmetics) + ASEAN ACD
enforcement_body: BPOM (Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan — Indonesian FDA)
key_requirements: Sunscreens require product notification with BPOM; UV filter positive list per BPOM Annex aligned with ASEAN ACD; SPF testing documentation; Bahasa Indonesia labeling; Halal certification consideration; BPOM registration number on label
notes: Indonesia: Halal certification increasingly important for cosmetics market (Halal Product Assurance Law enacted). UV filter positive list aligned with ASEAN ACD. BPOM notification process available online.

regulation_name: Indonesia Halal Certification for Cosmetics (JPH Law)
regulation_ref: Law No. 33 of 2014 on Halal Product Assurance (JPH) + PP No. 39/2021
key_requirements: Cosmetics including sunscreens sold in Indonesia must have halal certification or labeling declaring non-halal status; effective for cosmetics from 2026 onwards; UV filter source (animal-derived vs synthetic) must be documented
notes: Mandatory halal certification for cosmetics in Indonesia rolling out 2024-2026. Sunscreen UV filters of animal origin (e.g. certain extracts) require halal documentation. TiO2 and ZnO (mineral) are inherently halal.

key_requirements: Sunscreens as cosmetics: product notification with FDA Philippines; UV filter positive list per ASEAN ACD; SPF claim documentation; English/Filipino labeling; safety data on file; Responsible Person or local agent

regulation_name: Philippines DENR — Oxybenzone ban in marine protected areas
regulation_ref: Republic Act 11765 (2022) + DENR Administrative Order + local ordinances
enforcement_body: DENR (Dept of Environment and Natural Resources) + DILG
key_requirements: Ban on sale and use of sunscreens containing oxybenzone (benzophenone-3) and octinoxate in marine protected areas; civil/criminal penalties; signage at beach access points; enforcement in Tubbataha, Coron, and other MPAs
notes: Philippines has enacted national-level marine protected area restriction on oxybenzone/octinoxate — similar to Hawaii. RA 11765 (Financial Products and Services Consumer Protection Act) + environmental orders. Check current list of affected MPAs.

regulation_ref: Drugs and Cosmetics Act 1940 + New Cosmetics Rules 2020 (Schedule S)
enforcement_body: CDSCO (Central Drugs Standard Control Organisation) + State Licensing Authorities
key_requirements: Sunscreens regulated as cosmetics; import license required for foreign products; UV filter list per Schedule S of Cosmetics Rules 2020 (broadly aligned with EU); GMP compliance; English or local language labeling; cosmetic import license from CDSCO
notes: India Cosmetics Rules 2020 revised the framework. Sunscreens with SPF claims remain cosmetics (not drugs) in India. Import license needed. UV filter positive list in Schedule S.

regulation_name: TGA Sunscreen Standard (Therapeutic Goods) — Primary Standard
key_requirements: Sunscreens classified as therapeutic goods; must be listed or registered on ARTG; approved UV filter list in TGO 106; SPF testing per AS/NZS 2604:2012; UVA protection per ISO 24442; labeling per Therapeutic Goods Act; SPF 4-50+ range; water resistance testing; formulator must be Australian sponsor
notes: Critical AU specificity: sunscreens = therapeutic goods regulated by TGA (not cosmetics). Products must be ARTG-listed (AUST L) before sale. TGO 106 UV filter permitted list differs from EU and US — some EU filters not in TGO 106.

regulation_name: Standard for the Uniform Scheduling of Medicines and Poisons (SUSMP) — Sunscreen
regulation_ref: SUSMP (Poisons Standard) under Therapeutic Goods Act 1989
key_requirements: Schedule classification for sunscreen active ingredients; most UV filters unscheduled when used in sunscreen; controls on concentration limits for certain actives; ARTG listing confirmation required

regulation_name: AS/NZS 2604:2012 — Sunscreen Products: Testing and Labeling
key_requirements: Australian and NZ standard for SPF testing; in-vivo SPF testing protocol; water resistance; labeling SPF categories; primary standard referenced in TGO 106
notes: AS/NZS 2604 is the mandatory testing standard for TGA-listed sunscreens. SPF testing must follow this standard. Key divergence from ISO 24444 (EU) and FDA methods.

regulation_ref: AS/NZS 2604:2012 + Medicines Act 1981 / Therapeutic Products Act (TPA 2023)
enforcement_body: Medsafe (NZ Medicines and Medical Devices Safety Authority) + Ministry of Health
key_requirements: NZ treats primary sunscreens as medicines/therapeutic products; AS/NZS 2604:2012 testing; UV filter list aligned with AU TGO 106; ARTG-listed products accepted or NZ-specific registration; Therapeutic Products Act 2023 coming into force (phased)
notes: NZ aligning with AU therapeutic goods framework. Therapeutic Products Act 2023 will create new regulatory framework — transition period ongoing. Currently Medsafe oversees sunscreens under Medicines Act 1981.

regulation_name: ASEAN Cosmetics Directive (ACD) — Annex VI UV Filters
regulation_ref: ASEAN Cosmetics Directive (ACD) + Annex VI (UV Filters List)
key_requirements: Harmonized positive list of UV filters for ASEAN member states (mirrors EU Annex VI with ASEAN-specific updates); mutual recognition of cosmetics notifications between members (in principle); SPF testing method harmonization
notes: ASEAN ACD Annex VI UV filter list broadly mirrors EU Annex VI but may lag EU amendments. Some ASEAN countries (MY, ID, PH, TH, VN, SG) implement with country-specific deviations. ASEAN MRA for cosmetics not universally applied.

regulation_name: GSO 1943:2016 — GCC Standard for Sunscreen Products
enforcement_body: GSO + National Standards Bodies (SASO, ESMA, QSMO, BSB, ASO, MSB)
key_requirements: GCC regional standard for sunscreen products; UV filter list; SPF testing requirements; labeling in Arabic and English; halal compliance considerations; registration/notification per individual GCC country requirements
notes: Each GCC member implements GSO 1943 through national regulations. Saudi Arabia (SASO) most stringent — product registration required. UAE (ESMA) notification for cosmetics. UV filter list in GSO 1943 aligned broadly with EU Annex VI.

regulation_ref: GSO 2055-1:2015 (Halal — General Requirements) applied to cosmetics
key_requirements: Halal compliance for cosmetics sold in GCC; no porcine-derived ingredients; alcohol-free formulation preferred; halal certification from recognized body increasingly required for market access in KSA, UAE
notes: Halal certification increasingly required for cosmetics in GCC, particularly Saudi Arabia. UV filters sourced from synthetic chemistry (TiO2, ZnO, chemical UV filters) generally halal-compatible. Check specific filter sources.

regulation_name: Israel Standards Institution — SI 1651 Sunscreen Products
enforcement_body: Ministry of Health + ISAS (Israel Standards Authority)
key_requirements: Sunscreens classified as cosmetics in Israel; SI 1651 national standard for sunscreen testing and labeling; UV filter list aligned with EU Annex VI; product registration with Ministry of Health; Hebrew/Arabic labeling requirements
notes: Israel has adopted EU-aligned UV filter list. MoH registration required for import/marketing. SI 1651 references ISO testing methods. Post-Abraham Accords, some regulatory alignment with GCC countries.

regulation_ref: Egyptian Drug Authority Cosmetics Regulations + ES 3234 standard
key_requirements: Sunscreens require registration with Egyptian Drug Authority; UV filter list aligned broadly with EU; Arabic and English labeling; SPF testing documentation; local agent/importer required; product dossier submission
notes: Egypt EDA (formerly CAPA) handles cosmetics registration. Sunscreen registration mandatory before import. ES 3234 standard references ISO 24444 for SPF testing. Arabic labeling mandatory.

regulation_name: Morocco ONSSA/Ministry of Health — Cosmetics Regulation (Sunscreen)
regulation_ref: Loi 17-04 (Code du Médicament et de la Pharmacie, 2006) + Décret application cosmétiques
enforcement_body: Ministry of Health (Direction du Médicament et de la Pharmacie) + ONSSA
key_requirements: Sunscreens as cosmetics regulated under pharmaceutical code; product notification or registration depending on claims; UV filter list aligned with EU; French/Arabic labeling; local Responsible Person; SPF substantiation
notes: Morocco follows EU-aligned cosmetics framework. French and Arabic labeling required. EU-standard UV filter list adopted. Regulatory reform ongoing to align fully with EU 1223/2009.

regulation_name: SAHPRA — Cosmetics Regulation (Sunscreen) under Health Products Act
regulation_ref: Health Products Act 2 of 2024 + Cosmetics Regulations (SAHPRA) — in transition
enforcement_body: SAHPRA (South African Health Products Regulatory Authority)
key_requirements: Sunscreens transitioning to SAHPRA oversight under Health Products Act 2024; currently under Foodstuffs, Cosmetics and Disinfectants Act (54/1972) Regulation 628; UV filter list aligned with EU; English/Afrikaans labeling; safety notification
notes: South Africa: major regulatory transition underway. Health Products Act 2024 replaces Medicines and Related Substances Act for health products. Sunscreen classification (cosmetic vs drug) under review. Monitor SAHPRA implementation timeline.

regulation_name: South Africa FCD Act Regulation 628 — Cosmetics (current framework)
regulation_ref: Regulation 628 under Foodstuffs Cosmetics and Disinfectants Act 54/1972
key_requirements: Current operative framework: sunscreens as cosmetics; prohibited substances per Regulation 628 Schedules; UV filter list; no pre-market registration required (notification only); English labeling; SPF claim substantiation
notes: Regulation 628 remains operative until SAHPRA Health Products Act regulations fully activated. UV filter list in Reg 628 aligned with EU Annex VI.

enforcement_body: NAFDAC (National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control)
key_requirements: Sunscreens require NAFDAC registration; product dossier submission; UV filter list per NAFDAC cosmetics guidelines (broadly EU-aligned); English labeling; local agent or NAFDAC-registered importer; safety assessment
notes: NAFDAC registration mandatory before sunscreen marketing in Nigeria. Processing time can be 6-18 months. UV filter list broadly follows EU guidance. Fee structure varies by product category.

regulation_ref: Kenya Standards KS 2834 + Pharmacy and Poisons Act (Cosmetics) Regulations
enforcement_body: KEBS (Kenya Bureau of Standards) + Pharmacy and Poisons Board (PPB)
key_requirements: Sunscreens require registration with PPB and/or KEBS standardization mark; UV filter list aligned with international standards; English labeling; import permit; safety dossier; local agent required
notes: Kenya PPB handles sunscreen registration under cosmetics category. KEBS mark required for market entry. EAC (East African Community) harmonization in progress — may align with common EAC cosmetics standard.

regulation_name: ISO 24444:2019 — Cosmetics: In vivo SPF determination
key_requirements: International standard for in-vivo SPF testing; human volunteer panel; statistical methodology; referenced by EU (COLIPA method), CN (GB/T 35916), KR, AU; key for SPF claim substantiation
notes: ISO 24444 is the global de facto standard for SPF testing. US FDA uses its own method (21 CFR 201.327). AU uses AS/NZS 2604:2012. Sunscreen dossiers for multiple markets need testing per multiple methods.

regulation_name: ISO 24442:2011 — Cosmetics: In vivo UVA determination
key_requirements: International standard for in-vivo UVA protection (persistent pigment darkening method — PPD); required for UVA claim in EU, KR, CN; UVA/SPF ratio ≥1/3 required for EU broad spectrum UVA logo
notes: EU requires UVA claim per ISO 24442 or critical wavelength method. South Korea and China also reference ISO 24442. UVA protection labeling increasingly expected globally.

regulation_name: ISO 24443:2012 — Cosmetics: In vitro UVA determination
key_requirements: In vitro method for UVA protection determination; faster and cheaper than in-vivo PPD; used as screening method; some regulators (EU, CN) accept alongside ISO 24442
notes: ISO 24443 in vitro method increasingly accepted. EU industry uses as complementary method. Particularly useful for early formulation screening.

regulation_ref: ISO 16474:2013 (Methods of exposure to laboratory light sources)
key_requirements: Water resistance testing for sunscreen products; 40-min and 80-min water immersion protocols; referenced for water resistance claims; US FDA uses same protocol (21 CFR 201.327)
notes: Water resistance claims (40 min / 80 min) tested per ISO 16474 or equivalent. US, EU, AU all reference similar immersion test. Standard terminology: water resistant (40 min) vs very water resistant (80 min).

regulation_name: ISO 22716:2007 — Cosmetics GMP (applicable to sunscreen manufacturing)
key_requirements: GMP standard for cosmetics manufacturing; production, control, storage, shipment; referenced by EU, CN (NMPA), ASEAN, AU, ZA as basis for sunscreen GMP compliance
notes: Note: AU TGA sunscreens (therapeutic goods) require TGA manufacturing license, not just ISO 22716 GMP. US sunscreens (OTC drugs) require FDA cGMP 21 CFR 211.

regulation_name: Codex Alimentarius (not applicable — sunscreen is not a food)
key_requirements: Codex covers food safety standards; sunscreen as topical cosmetic/drug not in scope
notes: Not applicable to sunscreen. Included for completeness only.

key_requirements: UNECE WP.29 covers motor vehicle safety; not applicable to sunscreen cosmetics
notes: Not applicable. Included for completeness.

regulation_name: WADA Prohibited List (not applicable — sunscreen topical use)
key_requirements: WADA Prohibited List covers sports doping performance enhancing substances; topical sunscreen use not in scope; no listed UV filters
notes: Not applicable to sunscreen for normal consumer use.

regulation_name: OECD Test Guidelines — UV filter safety assessment
regulation_ref: OECD TG 428 (skin absorption), TG 406 (sensitisation), TG 471 (Ames test) + ED screening
key_requirements: OECD validated test methods required for UV filter safety data generation; skin absorption data for TiO2 nano and ZnO nano; photosensitisation testing; endocrine disruptor screening per OECD TG 455/457
notes: OECD TG 428 (skin absorption) critical for nano UV filters to assess systemic exposure. OECD ED testing guidelines (TG 455/457) used to assess endocrine disruption potential of chemical UV filters (oxybenzone, homosalate etc.).

regulation_name: EU Cosmetics Regulation — SPF Labeling Recommendation (COLIPA/EU Commission)
key_requirements: SPF categories: low (6-14), medium (15-29), high (30-59), very high (50+); maximum SPF value on label = tested SPF; UVA circle logo if UVA claim; no false sense of total protection claims; application instruction (2mg/cm²) on label
notes: Non-binding recommendation but de facto industry standard in EU. COLIPA/Cosmetics Europe SPF test protocol adopted widely. Regulators may enforce misleading claims under general product safety rules.

regulation_name: EU General Product Safety Regulation (GPSR) — Sunscreen market surveillance
key_requirements: General product safety requirements applicable to all consumer products including sunscreens; risk assessment; incident reporting via Safety Gate portal; online marketplace obligations; traceability
notes: GPSR replaced the General Product Safety Directive in December 2024. Market surveillance authorities may audit sunscreen SPF label accuracy and ingredient compliance.

regulation_name: EU Environmental Regulation — Sunscreen packaging (Packaging & Packaging Waste Regulation)
regulation_ref: Regulation (EU) 2025/40 (PPWR — Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation)
key_requirements: Recycled content requirements for plastic packaging; recyclability; minimization of unnecessary packaging; impacts sunscreen tube and bottle formats; extended producer responsibility
notes: PPWR adopted 2025, phased implementation 2028-2030. Sunscreen packaging (tubes, sprays) must meet recyclability and recycled content targets. Reef-safe packaging claims also trending.

regulation_name: US Virgin Islands — Reef-safe Sunscreen Act
enforcement_body: USVI Dept of Planning and Natural Resources
key_requirements: Ban on sale of sunscreens containing oxybenzone and octinoxate in US Virgin Islands; effective March 30, 2020; similar to Hawaii Act 104
notes: US territory — state-level OTC drug regulation generally preempted by FDA but ingredient-specific environmental bans allowed. Monitor Key West (FL) and other US coastal municipalities for similar ordinances.

regulation_name: FDA cGMP for OTC Drug Manufacturing — Sunscreen
regulation_ref: 21 CFR Part 211 (Current Good Manufacturing Practice for Finished Pharmaceuticals)
key_requirements: Sunscreen manufacturers must comply with pharmaceutical cGMP (21 CFR 211) — more stringent than cosmetics GMP; batch record requirements; out-of-specification investigation; stability testing; validated analytical methods for UV filter assay
notes: US OTC drug cGMP significantly stricter than cosmetics GMP (ISO 22716). Contract manufacturers for US market must have FDA-registered facilities with OTC drug cGMP. Key compliance gap for EU cosmetics manufacturers entering US market.

regulation_name: Canada Environmental Protection Act (CEPA) — UV filter environmental assessment
regulation_ref: Canadian Environmental Protection Act 1999 (CEPA) + Environment Canada assessments
key_requirements: Environmental assessment of UV filter chemicals entering aquatic environment via wash-off; oxybenzone aquatic toxicity assessment; some UV filters may be flagged as toxic under CEPA; may affect permitted use
notes: Canada CEPA assessments of UV filters ongoing. Oxybenzone flagged for aquatic toxicity in 2020 draft assessment. Potential restrictions on certain UV filters in sunscreens sold in Canada. Monitor ECCC substance assessments.

regulation_name: ANVISA Cosmetics GMP Resolution — Sunscreen Manufacturing
regulation_ref: RDC No. 301/2019 (Boas Práticas de Fabricação de Produtos de Higiene Pessoal, Cosméticos e Perfumes)
key_requirements: GMP requirements for sunscreen manufacturing; production documentation; quality control testing; stability; microbiological testing; UV filter assay methods; batch traceability

regulation_name: China CSAR — Sunscreen Efficacy Evaluation Guideline
regulation_ref: NMPA Guiding Principles for Efficacy Evaluation of Sunscreen Cosmetics (2022)
key_requirements: SPF testing must be conducted in China-qualified labs (CMA or CNAS accredited) or recognized foreign labs; in-vivo SPF per ISO 24444; UVA per ISO 24442; water resistance per ISO 16474; results submitted in CSAR registration dossier
notes: China requires SPF testing in NMPA-recognized laboratories. Foreign lab results may be accepted if lab is recognized. In-vivo testing with Chinese subjects sometimes required. Testing in China adds cost and timeline.

regulation_name: Japan SPF Testing and PA Rating System
regulation_ref: JCIA (Japan Cosmetic Industry Association) SPF Test Method + MHLW Quasi-drug labeling
key_requirements: Japan uses PA+++ rating system for UVA protection (PA+, PA++, PA+++, PA++++); SPF test per JCIA method or ISO 24444; PA rating based on PPD ratio; quasi-drug sunscreens must display PA and SPF on label; Japan market prefers high SPF/PA products
notes: PA system unique to Japan/Korea market. PA++++ = PPD ≥16. UVA claim in Japan via PA rating not EU UVA logo. Consumers in Asia expect PA+++ or PA++++ for daily use products.

regulation_name: Korea PA Rating and SPF Labeling Requirements
regulation_ref: MFDS Functional Cosmetics Standards + Cosmetics Act Labeling Regulation
key_requirements: PA rating (PA+/PA++/PA+++/PA++++) for UVA protection; SPF 2-50+ range; SPF and PA mandatory on functional cosmetics label; SPF testing per KS M 2503:2015 or ISO 24444; UVA per ISO 24442 or JCIA PPD method
notes: Korea uses same PA system as Japan. Both SPF and PA must appear on label. MFDS allows foreign test data if conducted in ISO/IEC 17025 accredited lab using recognized methodology.

regulation_name: TGA ARTG Listing Process — Sunscreen (AUST L)
regulation_ref: Therapeutic Goods Act 1989 + Therapeutic Goods (Listing Criteria) Order 2019
key_requirements: Sunscreens must be entered on ARTG as listed therapeutic goods (AUST L); sponsor (Australian company) responsible for listing; self-assessment of compliance with TGO 106; TGA may audit listings; fee per ARTG entry
notes: All sunscreens sold in Australia must have AUST L number on label and be current on ARTG. Sponsor must be Australian entity. Foreign manufacturers must have TGA-licensed Australian sponsor. TGA conducts random listing audits.

regulation_name: New Zealand Therapeutic Products Act 2023 — Sunscreen transition
regulation_ref: Therapeutic Products Act 2023 (TPA 2023) — implementation phased from 2026
key_requirements: New TPA 2023 will replace Medicines Act 1981; sunscreens (therapeutic products) will require new registration under TPA framework; transition period for existing ARTG/AU-listed products; Australian sponsors may need separate NZ registration post-transition
notes: TPA 2023 signals significant NZ regulatory change. Commencement of key provisions being phased. Monitor Medsafe implementation timeline. Products currently accepted under joint AU-NZ recognition may need separate NZ registration.

regulation_name: Saudi Arabia SASO Sunscreen Product Registration
regulation_ref: SASO Technical Regulation + Saudi Food and Drug Authority (SFDA) Cosmetics Regulation
key_requirements: Sunscreens require SFDA product registration (not just notification) in Saudi Arabia; UV filter compliance with GSO 1943; Arabic labeling mandatory; halal compliance; SPF testing documentation; Saudi agent required; IECEE CoC for imported products
notes: Saudi Arabia is most stringent GCC market for sunscreens. SFDA registration process can take 3-6 months. Label must be in Arabic. Halal certification increasingly expected.

regulation_name: Israel Cosmetics Regulation — New framework (2021 amendment)
regulation_ref: Cosmetics and Toiletries Supervision Law 1974 + Regulations Amendment 2021
key_requirements: Updated cosmetics framework; pre-market notification required; EU-aligned prohibited/restricted substance list; UV filter list per Ministry of Health Schedule; Hebrew and Arabic labeling; authorized importer responsible
notes: Israel 2021 amendment modernized cosmetics regulation closer to EU framework. Sunscreen UV filter list updated to align with EU Annex VI. Products must be notified to Ministry of Health database.

regulation_ref: SANS 1557:2012 (South African National Standard for Sunscreen Products)
enforcement_body: SABS (South African Bureau of Standards) + SAHPRA
key_requirements: National standard for sunscreen product testing and labeling; SPF testing per ISO 24444; UV filter positive list; labeling SPF categories; water resistance claims; referenced in regulatory compliance
notes: SANS 1557 is the SA national sunscreen standard. Broadly aligned with ISO 24444 and AS/NZS 2604. SAHPRA market surveillance may reference SANS 1557 for compliance checks.

regulation_name: India BIS Standards for Sunscreen — IS 14185
regulation_ref: IS 14185 (Bureau of Indian Standards — Sunscreen preparations)
key_requirements: Indian standard for sunscreen products; SPF testing methods; UV filter concentration limits; labeling; aligned with international ISO methods; increasingly referenced in regulatory guidance

regulation_ref: Import and Export (General) Regulations (Cap 60A) + Trade Descriptions Ordinance

key_requirements: Updated cosmetics ingredient safety list; prohibited and restricted substances aligned with EU; UV filter restrictions; labeling requirements including origin information; updated nano substance handling

regulation_ref: Republic Act 7394 (Consumer Act) + BPS PS IECEE (safety standard)
key_requirements: Philippine Standard (PS) mark for consumer electronics; safety testing; DTI-accredited lab

enforcement_body: DENR (Dept. of Environment and Natural Resources)
key_requirements: Formal producer responsibility for electronic waste; EPR programme; implementing rules 2023
notes: RA 11898 introduces formal producer responsibility; covers smartphones

regulation_ref: Electronics & IT Goods (Requirements for Compulsory Registration) Order 2012 + S.O. No. 1022(E)/2021
key_requirements: BIS registration (CRS); testing by BIS-recognised lab; BIS licence number on product; mobile phones mandatory since 2012

regulation_ref: Indian Wireless Telegraphy Act 1933 + WPC Licensing Regime
enforcement_body: WPC (Wireless Planning and Coordination Wing; DOT)
key_requirements: Equipment Type Approval (ETA) from WPC; frequency use compliance; import licence for radio equipment

regulation_ref: Indian Telegraph Act 1885 + TEC GR (Generic Requirements) 2019
key_requirements: Essential Requirements compliance; TEC type approval for devices connected to public network
EPR targets for producers; registration on EPR portal; collection targets (25% to 30% to 40% phased); authorised recyclers; WEEE label
notes: Revised rules 2022 significantly strengthen EPR

regulation_ref: Hazardous and Other Wastes (Management and Transboundary Movement) Rules 2016
key_requirements: Import restrictions on hazardous substances; partial RoHS alignment; substance restrictions in electrical goods
notes: India does not have a standalone RoHS regulation; BIS standards and HOW Rules provide partial coverage

regulation_name: DPDP Act 2023 — Digital Personal Data Protection
regulation_ref: Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 (No. 22 of 2023)
enforcement_body: Data Protection Board of India (pending establishment)
key_requirements: Consent-based data processing; purpose limitation; data fiduciary obligations; breach notification; significant data fiduciary for large processors
notes: Rules under DPDP Act expected 2024-2025; Act notified Aug 2023

key_requirements: RCM mark mandatory; compliance with ACMA radio standards (AS/NZS 4268; AS/NZS CISPR 32 etc.); SDoC or certification; importer/manufacturer registration
notes: RCM = combined Electrical Safety + Radio compliance mark

regulation_ref: Electrical Equipment Safety System (EESS) administered under state/territory EE Acts
key_requirements: Level 2 or Level 3 responsible supplier registration; in-scope equipment includes smartphones; IEC 62368-1 basis

regulation_ref: ARPANSA Radiation Protection Standard RPS3 (2002) + AS/NZS 2772.2
enforcement_body: ARPANSA (Australian Radiation Protection and Nuclear Safety Agency)
key_requirements: SAR limit 2.0 W/kg (10g averaged); ICNIRP aligned; test required for mobile devices

regulation_ref: Product Emissions Standards Act 2017 + MobileMuster voluntary programme
enforcement_body: DCCEEW (Dept. of Climate Change; Energy; Environment and Water)
key_requirements: National mobile phone recycling programme; battery take-back; industry-wide voluntary but de facto mandatory
notes: MobileMuster is Australia's national mobile phone recycling programme

enforcement_body: OAIC (Office of the Australian Information Commissioner)
key_requirements: Data handling for smartphone manufacturers; collection limitation; use limitation; security; access; notifiable data breaches (NDB) scheme
notes: Privacy Act reform bill (2024) may expand obligations

key_requirements: RCM mark (shared with AU) for radio compliance; ACMA standards recognised; compliance statement
notes: NZ and AU share RCM mark; NZ recognises AU radio standards

key_requirements: Electrical safety compliance; supplier registration; RCM mark covers electrical safety for low-voltage products

regulation_ref: Waste Minimisation Act 2008 + Product Stewardship Accreditation
key_requirements: Product stewardship scheme for e-waste; voluntary accreditation (MobileMuster participation); emerging mandatory scheme
notes: Mandatory product stewardship for e-waste under development 2023-2024

regulation_name: ATRC MRA — Telecom Equipment Mutual Recognition
regulation_ref: ASEAN MRA on Conformity Assessment for Telecommunications Equipment (ATRC MRA)
key_requirements: Facilitates type approval recognition between signatory ASEAN members; reduces duplicative testing
notes: MRA covers BN; MY; PH; SG; TH; VN; not all members equally active

regulation_ref: ASEAN Working Group on Technical Barriers to Trade (ASEAN WGTBT)
key_requirements: Ongoing harmonisation of RoHS-type substance restrictions across ASEAN; not yet binding single regulation
notes: Harmonisation aspirational; individual national laws still controlling

regulation_ref: GSO Unified Technical Regulation No. GSO 1900 (EMC) + GSO 6825 (Safety) + GSO 1859 (Radio)
enforcement_body: GSO (Gulf Standardisation Organisation) + SASO; ESMA; QSMO; KOWSMD
key_requirements: G-Mark mandatory for listed products; type testing in GSO-recognised lab; factory inspection; covers safety; EMC; radio; applied in SA; AE; QA; KW; OM; BH

regulation_name: SASO SABER Platform — Saudi Arabia Product Registration
key_requirements: SASO product registration on SABER electronic customs clearance platform; IECEE CB test report accepted; SASO CoC
notes: Saudi Arabia enforces through SABER platform

regulation_ref: TDRA (Telecommunications and Digital Government Regulatory Authority) Type Approval
key_requirements: Mandatory TA for UAE market; TDRA TA certificate; frequency bands compliance; SAR test

regulation_name: GCC RoHS — Restriction of Hazardous Substances
key_requirements: Substance restrictions aligned with EU RoHS 2; declaration; labelling
notes: GCC RoHS progressively enforced across member states

regulation_ref: Wireless Telegraph Ordinance (New Version) 5732 + MOC Approval Scheme
key_requirements: Mandatory MOC type approval for radio devices; MOC certificate; frequency compliance

enforcement_body: SII (Standards Institution of Israel) + ILAC mutual recognition
key_requirements: CE mark partially recognised with SII adaptation; IEC 62368-1 safety; CISPR 32 EMC; SII certification or declaration
notes: Israel often accepts CE + SII adaptation declaration

regulation_ref: Protection of Privacy Law 5741-1981 + PPPA Regulations 2017
key_requirements: Database registration; security requirements; data processing obligations; consumer device data
notes: Israel PPA reform bill expected 2024

regulation_ref: Telecommunications Regulation Law 10/2003 + NTRA Technical Regulation
key_requirements: Mandatory type approval; NTRA certificate; SAR test; frequency bands compliance

regulation_ref: Egyptian Organisation for Standardisation (EOS) + Egyptian Standards ES 62368
key_requirements: Safety conformity per ES 62368-1; CE mark partially accepted with NTRA approval
E-waste classified; producer responsibility emerging; Ministry guidance on e-waste management
notes: Implementation still nascent; enforcement variable

enforcement_body: ANRT (Agence Nationale de Réglementation des Télécommunications)
key_requirements: Mandatory ANRT homologation for radio equipment; ANRT certificate; frequency compliance; testing in ANRT-recognised lab

regulation_ref: Décret No. 2-00-1017 + IMANOR NM ISO/IEC standards
key_requirements: CE mark generally accepted; IMANOR NM standard for EEE safety; importer declaration

regulation_ref: Loi 09-08 relative à la protection des personnes physiques à l'égard du traitement des données
enforcement_body: CNDP (Commission Nationale de contrôle de la protection des données)
key_requirements: Data controller registration; consent; data subject rights; cross-border transfer authorisation

enforcement_body: ICASA (Independent Communications Authority of South Africa)
key_requirements: Mandatory ICASA type approval; TA number; SAR test; frequency compliance

regulation_ref: Compulsory Specification VC 8055 (Safety of EEE) + NRCS LoA
key_requirements: Letter of Authority (LoA) from NRCS; IEC 62368-1 safety; accredited lab testing

regulation_name: POPIA — Protection of Personal Information Act
regulation_ref: Protection of Personal Information Act 4/2013 (POPIA); effective 1 July 2021
key_requirements: Data processing conditions; purpose limitation; consent; data subject rights; security safeguards; applies to device data collection from ZA data subjects

regulation_ref: National Environmental Management: Waste Act 59/2008 (NEMWA) + EPR Regulations GNR.1184 of 2021
enforcement_body: DFFE (Dept. of Forestry; Fisheries and the Environment)
key_requirements: Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) for e-waste; producer registration with PROs (e-waste sector); take-back targets; phones covered
notes: EPR for e-waste came into force 5 Nov 2021 in ZA

regulation_ref: Nigerian Communications Act 2003 + NCC Type Approval Regulations 2015
key_requirements: Mandatory type approval; NCC TA certificate; SAR limits; frequency compliance

regulation_ref: Standards Organisation of Nigeria Act + SON-IEC 62368
key_requirements: Safety certification; SON conformity mark; testing in SON-recognised lab

regulation_ref: National Environmental (Electrical/Electronic Sector) Regulations 2011 S.I. No. 23
enforcement_body: NESREA (National Environmental Standards and Regulations Enforcement Agency)
key_requirements: Restriction of hazardous substances in EEE; import prohibition on substandard e-waste; producer responsibility emerging

regulation_ref: Kenya Information and Communications Act (KICA) Cap 411A + CA Type Approval Rules 2010
key_requirements: Mandatory type approval; CA certificate; frequency compliance; SAR testing

regulation_ref: Kenya Bureau of Standards + Diamond Mark of Quality
key_requirements: KEBS standardisation mark; safety based on IEC 62368-1 (adoption); import certification

regulation_ref: Environmental Management and Co-ordination Act (EMCA) 1999 + NEMA E-Waste Regulations 2021
key_requirements: E-waste management regulations; producer/importer registration; take-back; recycler authorisation

enforcement_body: ODPC (Office of the Data Protection Commissioner)
key_requirements: Data processor/controller registration; consent; data subject rights; cross-border transfer conditions; security measures
notes: ODPC established 2021; enforcement ramping up

key_requirements: ISMS certification; risk management; security controls (Annex A); device security aspects; supply chain security; applicable to device OEMs operations
notes: Voluntary standard; increasingly contractually required by enterprise buyers and carriers

key_requirements: Hazard-based safety engineering standard; basis for national safety certifications (UL 62368-1; EN 62368-1; GB 4943.1 etc.); replaces IEC 60950-1 and IEC 60065
notes: Global harmonised safety standard; basis for 95%+ national requirements

key_requirements: 13 provisions for IoT baseline security: no universal default passwords; vulnerability disclosure; software updates; data minimisation; secure communications; basis for EU CRA; UK PSTI; CA SB-327
notes: Widely referenced globally as IoT security baseline

key_requirements: Technical specifications for radio protocols; SAR measurement methodologies (IEC 62209); OTA performance requirements; referenced by all national type approval schemes
notes: Technical foundations for all telecom certifications

regulation_name: ITU Radio Regulations + ITU-T E.212 — IMEI
key_requirements: IMEI structure and allocation (TAC assigned by GSMA); ITU frequency band allocations (WRC decisions); international numbering plan

enforcement_body: EC DG GROW + National Market Surveillance Authorities
key_requirements: CE marking mandatory; essential safety requirements; mechanical & physical safety; flammability; chemical safety; age warning ≥ 36 months or small parts warning; EN 71 harmonised standards; DOC (Declaration of Conformity)
notes: Fundamental EU framework for toys; applies in all 27 member states; small parts test critical for 0-3 ans

key_requirements: Small parts cylinder test (must retain objects >31.7mm diameter for <3 years); tensile strength of eyes/nose/buttons; bite force test; age grading; squeeze toy safety; no accessible sharp edges or points
notes: EN 71-1 is the cornerstone standard for plush toys targeting 0-3 ans; small parts the #1 critical point

key_requirements: Plush/soft toys must not be easily ignitable; burn rate limits; prohibition on highly flammable materials in contact with face/hair/body; specific test for pile fabrics
notes: Peluche fabric and stuffing must meet flammability criteria; special concern for synthetic pile

regulation_name: EN 71-3 — Migration of Certain Elements (Heavy Metals)
key_requirements: Migration limits for Sb Ar Ba Cd Cr Co Cu Pb Mn Hg Ni Se Sr Sn Zn in toy materials by material category (dry; brittle; powder; liquid); strictest limits for Category I (scraped materials) applicable to plush pile
notes: Critical for dyes and coatings on plush fur; lead and cadmium limits extremely strict for Category intended for children <36 months

key_requirements: Limits on nitrosamines; formaldehyde; preservatives (e.g. MIT/CMIT); azo dyes; restricted solvents in toy materials; applies to fabric and stuffing
notes: Formaldehyde from fabric finishing relevant for plush; azo dyes restrictions for coloured plush

regulation_ref: REACH Annex XVII Entries 51 & 52 (Reg. 1907/2006 as amended)
key_requirements: DEHP+DBP+BBP ≤0.1% in toys accessible to children; DINP+DIDP+DNOP ≤0.1% in toys for children <3 or intended to be mouthed; applies to all plastic components including zippers and PE stuffing if plasticised
notes: Peluche may contain plastic components; stuffing pellets sometimes plasticised; check all plastic trims

regulation_name: REACH SVHC — Substances of Very High Concern
key_requirements: Communication obligation if SVHC >0.1% w/w; consumer right to information within 45 days; ongoing updates to SVHC list (~240 substances); duty to notify ECHA Article 7 if SVHC in article >1t/year
notes: Applies to all materials in plush toy; flame retardants and dyes of particular concern

regulation_ref: COM(2023) 462 final — proposed Toy Safety Regulation
key_requirements: Proposed replacement for Directive 2009/48/EC; direct regulation (no national transposition); stricter chemical requirements; expanded scope for digital features; mandatory third-party conformity assessment for chemical requirements; enhanced traceability via digital product passport
notes: Legislative process ongoing 2024-2026; monitor for adoption; may tighten chemical limits significantly

key_requirements: CE marking; déclaration UE de conformité; notice en français pour jouets avec risques; tranche d'âge obligatoire sur emballage; surveillance du marché par DGCCRF
notes: Transposition directe; la DGCCRF mène des campagnes de contrôle annuelles sur les jouets

regulation_name: Code de la consommation — étiquetage jouets
regulation_ref: Articles L423-1 à L423-5 et R423-1+ du Code de la consommation
key_requirements: Mention age (<36 mois: petites pièces) obligatoire; avertissements de sécurité en français; identification du fabricant/importateur; traçabilité lot
notes: Exigences d'étiquetage en langue française; mention «Ne convient pas aux enfants de moins de 36 mois» si petites pièces détachables

enforcement_body: BAuA (Bundesanstalt für Arbeitsschutz und Arbeitsmedizin) + Marktüberwachungsbehörden der Länder
key_requirements: CE-Kennzeichnung; technische Dokumentation; Konformitätserklärung; deutsche Gebrauchsanweisung; GS-Zeichen (freiwillig); Altershinweise auf Verpackung
notes: Germany has strong market surveillance; GS mark (geprüfte Sicherheit) voluntary but commercially important

key_requirements: National ban on specific azo dyes (already in REACH but nationally reinforced); nickel release limits; formaldehyde limits in textiles (relevant for plush fabric)
notes: BfR publishes opinions on toy chemicals; German market surveillance particularly active on chemical content

enforcement_body: Ministero dello Sviluppo Economico + Guardia di Finanza
key_requirements: Marcatura CE; documentazione tecnica; avvertenze in italiano; controllo di mercato; notifica incidenti
notes: Italy is significant toy market (Chicco HQ); active market surveillance

key_requirements: Migration limits heavy metals; phthalate restrictions; azo dye ban; SVHC communication; Italian NCA coordinates with ECHA
notes: Italian enforcement notably active on chemical substances in toys marketed to young children

key_requirements: Marcado CE; declaración UE de conformidad; instrucciones en español; advertencias de edad; vigilancia del mercado
notes: Spain transposes via RD 1205/2011; CCAA (autonomous communities) carry out market surveillance

key_requirements: Restricciones ftalatos; metales pesados EN 71-3; comunicación SVHC; control de compuestos orgánicos
notes: AEMPS coordinates market surveillance for chemical substances in toys

key_requirements: CE-markering; technische documentatie; NL-taalvereisten instructies; leeftijdswaarschuwing op verpakking; speelgoedspecifieke chemische eisen
notes: NVWA conducts regular toy safety checks; Netherlands is major import hub (Rotterdam port)

regulation_name: REACH handhaving speelgoed — ftalaten en SVHC
key_requirements: Controle ftalaten in plastic onderdelen; SVHC>0.1% communicatieplicht; azo-kleurstoffen verbod
notes: Netherlands port authority (Rotterdam) is key point for toy imports into EU; NVWA intercepts non-compliant toys

enforcement_body: OPSS (Office for Product Safety and Standards)
key_requirements: UKCA marking (mandatory since 1 Jan 2025); UK DoC; technical file; English-language instructions and warnings; age grading; small parts warning for <3 years
notes: Post-Brexit: UKCA replaces CE for GB market; NI may retain CE under Windsor Framework; separate GB and NI compliance tracks

regulation_ref: UK REACH (retained Reg. 1907/2006) + UK Annex XVII
key_requirements: UK phthalate limits mirror EU: DEHP+DBP+BBP ≤0.1%; DINP+DIDP+DNOP ≤0.1% for mouthable toys for <3; heavy metal migration limits retained EN 71-3
notes: UK REACH diverging from EU REACH; monitor for amendments; HSE maintains UK substance restriction list

regulation_name: BS EN 71 series — UK adopted standards
regulation_ref: BS EN 71-1:2014+A1:2018; BS EN 71-2; BS EN 71-3 (UK adoption)
key_requirements: Same technical requirements as EU EN 71; small parts test; flammability; chemical migration; UK still aligned post-Brexit but may diverge
notes: BSI adopts EN standards; UK market still accepts EN 71 test reports from accredited labs

regulation_ref: SR 817.023.11 — Verordnung über die Sicherheit von Spielzeug (SpV) 2010
key_requirements: CE marking accepted; CH declaration of conformity required for CH market; MRA with EU means CE+DoC generally sufficient; Swiss-specific language requirements (DE/FR/IT) for warnings
notes: Switzerland-EU MRA means EN 71 / CE marking generally accepted; watch for deviations in labeling language

regulation_ref: SR 814.81 — Verordnung über das Verbot gefährlicher Stoffe
key_requirements: Phthalate restrictions aligned with EU REACH; nickel release limits; azo dye restrictions; CMR substances
notes: Swiss chemical restrictions largely harmonised with EU; occasional stricter national limits

enforcement_body: Direktoratet for samfunnssikkerhet og beredskap (DSB) + Forbrukertilsynet
key_requirements: CE marking; Norwegian-language warnings; age grading; EN 71 harmonised standards; DSB market surveillance
notes: Norway EEA member; EU Toy Safety Directive applies via EEA Agreement; Norwegian language required for warnings

key_requirements: Phthalate restrictions; SVHC communication; heavy metal migration limits; azo dye restrictions
notes: Norway adopts EU REACH via EEA; enforcement by Norwegian Environment Agency

key_requirements: EAC marking (единый знак обращения на рынке ЕАЭС); conformity assessment: state registration for toys for <3 years (stricter than declaration); chemical migration limits; small parts requirements; age labeling in Russian
notes: Russia member of EAEU; TR TS 008/2011 applies; toys for children <3 years require mandatory state registration (more stringent than EC declaration)

regulation_name: GOST R 71-1/71-3 — Russian national toy standards
key_requirements: Mechanical and physical safety; chemical migration limits for heavy metals; small parts; Russian labeling
notes: GOST standards adopted and referenced in TR TS 008/2011 technical annex

regulation_name: Oyuncak Yönetmeliği — Turkish Toy Safety Regulation
regulation_ref: Oyuncak Güvenliği Yönetmeliği (2016/17/AB) — 27 Şubat 2016 tarihli R.G.
key_requirements: CE marking accepted; TS EN 71 series applicable; Turkish-language warnings mandatory; age grading; small parts warning; chemical requirements per EN 71-3
notes: Turkey aligned with EU Toy Safety Directive; CE marking accepted but Turkish language labeling required

regulation_ref: TS EN 71-1; TS EN 71-2; TS EN 71-3 (TSE adoption)
key_requirements: Same requirements as EU EN 71; TSE mark (voluntary) commercially important; mandatory for some categories
notes: TSE mark provides additional market confidence in Turkish market

regulation_ref: Постанова КМУ № 151 від 28.02.2018 (Технічний регламент безпечності іграшок)
key_requirements: Marking of conformity (Ukraine); declaration of conformity; age grading; small parts warning; Ukrainian language labeling; EN 71 standards referenced
notes: Ukraine aligning toy safety with EU directives; currently partially enforced due to conflict; EU Association Agreement framework

key_requirements: Phthalate restrictions; heavy metal migration; azo dye restrictions; aligned with EU REACH
notes: Ukraine implementing EU-aligned chemical restrictions; enforcement capacity limited

regulation_name: TR TS 008/2011 — Règlement technique EAEU Jouets
key_requirements: EAC marking; conformity assessment: déclaration for toys ≥3 ans; state registration certificate for toys <3 ans; age labeling; small parts test; chemical migration limits; flammability
notes: Covers Russia Kazakhstan Belarus Armenia Kyrgyzstan; toys for <3 years require state registration — most stringent in this bloc

regulation_ref: TR TS 008/2011 Annex + TR EAEU 041/2017 (chemical prod.)
key_requirements: Phthalate limits; heavy metal migration; formaldehyde limits in textile components; azo dye restrictions; aligned broadly with EU but with some differences
notes: EAEU chemical restrictions generally aligned with EU but not identical; verify specific limits for stuffing and fabric

regulation_name: CPSIA 2008 — Consumer Product Safety Improvement Act
key_requirements: Third-party conformity testing mandatory; Children's Product Certificate (CPC) required; lead content limit 100 ppm total / 90 ppm paint; phthalate limits (DEHP DBP BBP DIBP ≤0.1%); tracking label on product; CPSC registration; general conformity certificate
notes: CPSIA is the cornerstone US children's product law; third-party lab testing from CPSC-accepted lab mandatory; CPC must accompany every shipment

regulation_name: ASTM F963 — Standard Consumer Safety Spec for Toy Safety
key_requirements: Small parts cylinder test; fabric flammability; stuffed toy filling safety; surface coating lead; mechanical strength of attachments (eyes nose buttons); bite force test; acoustic toys; age grading label
notes: ASTM F963 is the primary voluntary (but legally mandated via CPSIA Section 106) toy safety standard in US; covers plush toys specifically

regulation_name: 16 CFR Part 1501 — Small Parts Test Methodology
key_requirements: Test cylinder dimensions 31.7mm x 57.1mm; any toy or component that fits entirely within cylinder is a small part; prohibited for toys for children <3 years; also applies to detachable components of plush toys
notes: Critical for plush: eyes nose buttons accessories must not detach and pass small parts cylinder; applies to all components in toys for children <3

regulation_name: 16 CFR Part 1303 — Lead in Surface Coatings
key_requirements: ≤90 ppm lead in surface coatings (paint varnish lacquer); applies to all painted/coated components of children's toys
notes: Applies to any painted accessories on plush (plastic eyes embroidered with metallic thread etc.); pre-2011 limit was 600 ppm

regulation_name: 16 CFR Part 1307 — Phthalates in Children's Toys
key_requirements: DEHP DBP BBP DIBP ≤0.1% permanently banned; DINP DPENP DHEXP DCHP DIPP ≤0.1% interim prohibited; applies to plasticised vinyl and plastic components in toys for children <12
notes: Applies to any plastic/vinyl component in plush toy (zippers toggles squeakers pellets)

regulation_name: 16 CFR Part 1500 — Hazardous Substances Act — Toy Provisions
key_requirements: Banned hazardous substances in toys; flammability test 16 CFR 1500.44 for fabrics; sharp points/edges test; electrical hazards
notes: Fabric flammability via 16 CFR 1500.44 for plush outer fabric and stuffing

key_requirements: CPC documents: product identity; applicable rules; date and place of manufacture; lab name and address; date and place of testing; importer or domestic manufacturer info. Tracking label: manufacturer name + location + date + batch
notes: Every plush toy sold in US for children <12 requires CPC; tracking label must be permanent (sewn-in label); critical for recalls

regulation_ref: Cal. Health & Safety Code §25249.5 et seq. (OEHHA)
enforcement_body: California Office of Environmental Health Hazard Assessment (OEHHA)
key_requirements: Warning label required if product exposes consumers to listed carcinogens or reproductive toxins above safe harbor levels; lead Cd phthalates formaldehyde commonly trigger Prop 65 for toys; safe harbor levels: lead 0.5 μg/day DEHP 780 μg/day
notes: Proposition 65 regularly triggers warnings for children's plush toys due to lead (dyes accessories) and phthalates; major litigation risk in California

regulation_name: California AB 1108 — Phthalates in Children's Products
regulation_ref: California Health & Safety Code §108935 et seq. (now superseded by CPSIA federally)
key_requirements: California phthalate restrictions now preempted by CPSIA federal rules; however DTSC SAFER Consumer Products program may impose additional requirements
notes: CPSIA federal phthalate rules preempt California AB 1108 in most cases; DTSC SAFER program monitoring ongoing for additional restrictions

regulation_name: New York Child Safe Products Act (CSPA)
regulation_ref: New York Environmental Conservation Law Article 37 (ECL §37-0101)
key_requirements: Reporting obligations for priority chemicals in children's products; manufacturers must report use of chemicals of concern in children's products; covers toys for children <12 years; lead phthalates flame retardants BPA commonly reported
notes: NY CSPA imposes reporting obligations beyond federal CPSIA; manufacturers must report and eventually phase out priority chemicals in children's toys

regulation_ref: SOR/2011-17 — Toys Regulations under Canada Consumer Product Safety Act
key_requirements: Age grading; small parts; labeling in English and French; chemical migration limits; heavy metal migration; phthalate restrictions; stuffed toy filling must be sanitized; CE or safety standards reference
notes: Canada's toy regulations cover small parts (identical cylinder test as US); bilingual EN/FR labeling mandatory; stuffing must be sanitary material

key_requirements: General prohibition on products that pose unreasonable danger; mandatory incident reporting; recall authority; market surveillance; overlapping with Toys Regulations SOR/2011-17
notes: CCPSA provides general safety framework; Health Canada actively recalls non-compliant toys

key_requirements: Lead in surface coatings ≤90 mg/kg (90 ppm); applies to painted components of toys; same level as US 16 CFR 1303
notes: Same lead limit as US; applies to painted plastic eyes and accessories on plush toys

regulation_name: NOM-015-SCFI-2007 — Información y etiquetado de juguetes
key_requirements: Age warning in Spanish; safety warnings for small parts; general labeling in Spanish: manufacturer name country of origin age grade; CE not automatically recognised — NOM compliance required for Mexican market
